title: CIFR's Bitcoin Mining Portfolio Grows: A Sign for More Upside?
text: Cipher Mining (CIFR) has significantly expanded its Bitcoin mining operations during Q3 2025. The company increased mining capacity from 423 to 477 megawatts across five sites, achieving a self-mining hash rate of approximately 23.6 exahash per second. Key developments include the full operationalization of the Black Pearl site, which contributed 10.1 exahash per second and accounted for 36% of quarterly production. The Odessa site remained the largest contributor at 56% of Bitcoin production. In Q3, CIFR mined 629 Bitcoin across wholly owned sites, generating $72 million in revenues. CIFR faces intense competition from larger competitors. Riot Platform produced 1,406 Bitcoin, generating $160.8 million in Bitcoin mining revenue during the same period, while Hut 8 reported revenues of $83.5 million with 91% year-over-year growth. Despite a 213.8% year-to-date stock surge, analysts note CIFR appears overvalued. The company's forward 12-month Price/Sales ratio of 15.69X significantly exceeds the Technology Services industry average of 2.54X. CIFR currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating with a 2025 loss projection of 37 cents per share.
